finding: runner-sync build timing currently appears as terminal output or ad hoc operator measurement, not as a first-class telemetry event/queryable receipt field for trend analysis.
expectedBehavior: Every runner-sync build should emit structured timing telemetry with cacheHitSkip, incrementalBuild, fullRebuild, phaseTimingsMs, output file count, input/output digest, sealed source SHA, steward work id, and exit status.
evidenceOrRepro: During ATM-GOV-0187 closeout, npm run build produced a manual seed metric: elapsedMs=48128, mode=cache-miss full, sealedSourceSha=313a8151abb4b09fe1fc55df53f8973f3899c7c6, stewardWorkId=runner-sync-19213019. The runner-sync release receipt was not cleanly committed by the closeback flow.
followUp: Fold this into ATM-GOV-0194 and/or a dedicated runner telemetry task so future task cards can consume cumulative build-speed data before deciding whether to optimize, defer, or rewrite scope.
